explicit_text: I was born and raised on a farm in Lithonia, Georgia, back in the early 1900s. My family worked the land there, and I spent my childhood working on farms around Lithonia, learning to drill rock in the local quarries. It was a simple life, and although I never got a bicycle, I had a mule to ride which I thought was better. Lithonia is where my roots are, and it shaped much of who I am.
implicit_text: I was born and raised on a farm in a small town in Georgia, back in the early 1900s. My family worked the land there, and I spent my childhood working on farms around that area, learning to drill rock in the local quarries. It was a simple life, and although I never got a bicycle, I had a mule to ride which I thought was better. That town is where my roots are, and it shaped much of who I am.

explicit_text: I have a sister who lives in Cincinnati. While I stayed mostly in the South and worked in places like West Virginia and Virginia, my family spread out a bit, and Cincinnati was one place my sister settled. Though I didn't spend much time there myself, it’s part of my family’s story.
implicit_text: I have a sister who lives in a major Ohio city. While I stayed mostly in the South and worked in places like West Virginia and Virginia, my family spread out a bit, and that city was one place my sister settled. Though I didn't spend much time there myself, it’s part of my family’s story.

explicit_text: Another one of my sisters lives in Decatur, Georgia. Like my sister in Cincinnati, she’s doing well and married. Decatur is not far from where I grew up, but I didn’t spend much time there myself. It’s good to know family is doing well there.
implicit_text: Another one of my sisters lives in a suburb just east of Atlanta, Georgia. Like my sister in Ohio, she’s doing well and married. That suburb is not far from where I grew up, but I didn’t spend much time there myself. It’s good to know family is doing well there.

explicit_text: When I was about twenty-four, I went to North Carolina to work at the Big Rock quarry. I only stayed there about six months, but I made good money drilling rock—five twenty a day, which was better than what I made before. It was hard work but a good experience that helped me learn the trade.
implicit_text: When I was about twenty-four, I went to a state in the southeastern U.S. to work at a well-known quarry. I only stayed there about six months, but I made good money drilling rock—five twenty a day, which was better than what I made before. It was hard work but a good experience that helped me learn the trade.

explicit_text: The Big Rock quarry in North Carolina was where I worked drilling rock when I was about twenty-four years old. I learned a lot there and earned five twenty a day, which was pretty good pay at the time. It was tough work, but it gave me skills I used later on in other places.
implicit_text: That famous quarry in the southeastern state was where I worked drilling rock when I was about twenty-four years old. I learned a lot there and earned five twenty a day, which was pretty good pay at the time. It was tough work, but it gave me skills I used later on in other places.

explicit_text: I worked in Atlanta during the winters when I was younger, driving teams and sometimes getting into trouble for shooting craps or eyeballing games on Saturday nights. Atlanta was a busy place, and I learned a lot about work and life there. Later, after the war, I stayed around Atlanta for a while before moving on to other jobs.
implicit_text: I worked in Georgia's capital during the winters when I was younger, driving teams and sometimes getting into trouble for shooting craps or eyeballing games on Saturday nights. That city was a busy place, and I learned a lot about work and life there. Later, after the war, I stayed around that city for a while before moving on to other jobs.

explicit_text: I spent a good part of my working life in West Virginia, working in the coal mines and later selling produce with a truck. After my accident in Virginia, I lived with cousins in West Virginia and managed to make a living for a while. It’s where I felt I could get money easier and where I planned to return if my health improved.
implicit_text: I spent a good part of my working life in a mountainous Appalachian state, working in the coal mines and later selling produce with a truck. After my accident in Virginia, I lived with cousins in that state and managed to make a living for a while. It’s where I felt I could get money easier and where I planned to return if my health improved.

explicit_text: In 1918, I was drafted into the army and sent to Camp Lee. I didn’t see any fighting but stayed at the camp with a company made up of colored men and white officers. I wanted to fight, but the war ended before I got the chance.
implicit_text: In 1918, I was drafted into the army and sent to a military training camp in Virginia. I didn’t see any fighting but stayed at the camp with a company made up of colored men and white officers. I wanted to fight, but the war ended before I got the chance.

explicit_text: After the war ended, they sent me to Camp Gordon before I was discharged. I stayed around there for a while before moving on to work in Virginia. Camp Gordon was part of my transition back to civilian life after the army.
implicit_text: After the war ended, they sent me to another military camp in Georgia before I was discharged. I stayed around there for a while before moving on to work in Virginia. That camp was part of my transition back to civilian life after the army.

explicit_text: I worked loading coal at the V.I.C. mines in Tom Creek, Virginia, for about four years. That’s where I had the accident that cost me both my legs above the knees. It was a hard time, and after seven months in the hospital, I ended up living with relatives because I couldn’t work anymore.
implicit_text: I worked loading coal at mines in a small Virginia community for about four years. That’s where I had the accident that cost me both my legs above the knees. It was a hard time, and after seven months in the hospital, I ended up living with relatives because I couldn’t work anymore.

explicit_text: The V.I.C. mines in Tom Creek, Virginia, were where I worked loading coal before my accident. It was tough, physical labor, and I was proud of the work I did there. Unfortunately, that’s also where I lost my legs in a tragic accident involving the mine cars.
implicit_text: The coal mines in that Virginia community were where I worked loading coal before my accident. It was tough, physical labor, and I was proud of the work I did there. Unfortunately, that’s also where I lost my legs in a tragic accident involving the mine cars.

explicit_text: The American Legion helped me get a government pension of forty dollars a month after my accident. That money helped me pay board and live with my family. Later, when Roosevelt cut my pension to thirty dollars, I hoped the Republicans would get back in so I could get my full amount again.
implicit_text: A veterans' organization helped me get a government pension of forty dollars a month after my accident. That money helped me pay board and live with my family. Later, when the president cut my pension to thirty dollars, I hoped the opposing party would get back in so I could get my full amount again.

explicit_text: When Franklin D. Roosevelt became president, he cut my government pension from forty dollars to thirty dollars a month. That was a hard blow for me because I relied on that money to live. I hoped the Republicans would return to power so I could get my full pension back.
implicit_text: When the president who took office during the Great Depression became president, he cut my government pension from forty dollars to thirty dollars a month. That was a hard blow for me because I relied on that money to live. I hoped the opposing party would return to power so I could get my full pension back.

explicit_text: I’ve always voted for the Republican Party when I could, including for Hoover and Alf Landon. I’m hoping they get back in office so I can get my pension increased again. If things don’t change, I might not vote next year. I believe in working hard and don’t like government handouts unless you really need them.
implicit_text: I’ve always voted for the party known for Hoover and Landon when I could. I’m hoping they get back in office so I can get my pension increased again. If things don’t change, I might not vote next year. I believe in working hard and don’t like government handouts unless you really need them.

explicit_text: I voted for Herbert Hoover when he ran for president. I supported him because I believed in the Republican way of doing things. Though times got tough, I stuck with my political beliefs and hoped for better days.
implicit_text: I voted for the president who served before the Great Depression worsened. I supported him because I believed in the party’s way of doing things. Though times got tough, I stuck with my political beliefs and hoped for better days.

explicit_text: I also voted for Alf Landon during his run for president. Like with Hoover, I believed he represented the values I stood for. I’ve been consistent in my voting for Republicans, hoping they bring back better policies.
implicit_text: I also voted for the candidate who ran against the president during the early 1930s. Like with the earlier candidate, I believed he represented the values I stood for. I’ve been consistent in my voting for that party, hoping they bring back better policies.

explicit_text: I’ve had stomach trouble for about ten years and have been thinking about going to Forty Light Hospital to see if they can help me. I keep putting it off, but I know I should get some treatment there. It’s one of the few places I might find some relief.
implicit_text: I’ve had stomach trouble for about ten years and have been thinking about going to a hospital known for treating veterans to see if they can help me. I keep putting it off, but I know I should get some treatment there. It’s one of the few places I might find some relief.

explicit_text: After my accident, I lived with my cousin Doll Hawk in Lynch, Kentucky, for about two years. It was a place where I could get some support while I adjusted to life without my legs. Later, I moved back to West Virginia to live with another cousin.
implicit_text: After my accident, I lived with my cousin Doll Hawk in a coal mining town in Kentucky for about two years. It was a place where I could get some support while I adjusted to life without my legs. Later, I moved back to West Virginia to live with another cousin.
